name: DocuSign
description: Electronic signature and agreement management platform. Yields envelope audit trails and Certificates of Completion, account security settings, user and permission profile reports, and security event data via DocuSign Monitor.

steps.action: Sign in to DocuSign eSignature, open Agreements, locate the completed envelopes in scope, then open each envelope's History and download the Certificate of Completion PDF
steps.permissions_required: DS Sender (own envelopes) or DS Admin (account-wide access)
steps.audit_note: The Certificate of Completion is generated by DocuSign and records signer identity, email, IP address, timestamps and envelope ID — it is strong, tamper-evident evidence of signature events

steps.action: Navigate to the Reports tab and run the envelope and user activity reports for the collection period, then select Download CSV
steps.permissions_required: DS Admin, or a permission profile with Reporting enabled
steps.audit_note: Set the report date range to cover the full period with no gaps between collections

steps.action: Navigate to Settings (eSignature Admin) > Security Settings and capture a screenshot of session timeout, password strength and login requirements
steps.audit_note: Include the browser URL and system clock in the screenshot so the capture date is verifiable

steps.action: Navigate to Settings > Users and export the user list, then capture Settings > Permission Profiles showing each profile and its member count
steps.security_note: The user export contains names and email addresses — store it in the evidence repository with restricted access

steps.action: Upload the Certificates of Completion, CSV exports and screenshots to the evidence repository with date-stamped filenames and evidence ID references
steps.security_note: Review envelope documents before upload — completed agreements may contain personal or commercially sensitive data that should not enter the evidence repository

steps.action: Navigate to the Reports tab, open the envelope or user activity report you need, and customise the columns and filters to match your evidence requirement
steps.permissions_required: DS Admin, or a permission profile with Reporting enabled

steps.action: Select Schedule on the report, set the recurrence (e.g., weekly on Monday) and add the recipients who should receive the emailed CSV
steps.audit_note: Scheduled reports are generated by DocuSign on the configured cadence and carry a generation timestamp

steps.action: Restrict the report recipients to a shared compliance mailbox rather than individual inboxes
steps.security_note: Report emails contain envelope metadata and user details — limit recipients to authorised personnel and use a mailbox with access controls

steps.action: In Settings > Signing Settings > Envelope Delivery, enable 'Attach certificate of completion to envelope' so every completed-document PDF automatically includes its certificate
steps.audit_note: This ensures the audit trail travels with the signed document itself, so archived agreements are self-evidencing

steps.action: Configure a mailbox rule or lightweight automation to file the emailed CSVs into the evidence repository on arrival
steps.audit_note: Filing on arrival avoids gaps caused by manual handling; verify the first two scheduled deliveries arrive as expected

steps.action: In DocuSign Admin, navigate to Settings > Apps and Keys, create an integration key for a dedicated service user and generate an RSA keypair for JWT Grant authentication
steps.security_note: Use a dedicated service user, not a personal account. Store the RSA private key in a secrets manager, never in source control.

steps.action: Grant one-time consent for the signature and impersonation scopes, then have the script exchange the signed JWT for an access token at the OAuth token endpoint
steps.permissions_required: Service user with envelope visibility; DS Admin to grant admin-level consent
steps.security_note: Scope the service user to the minimum permission profile needed for reading envelope data

steps.action: Call GET /restapi/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/envelopes with from_date and status filters (Envelopes:listStatusChanges) to enumerate envelopes completed since the last run
steps.permissions_required: Access token for the service user
steps.audit_note: Persist the last from_date watermark so consecutive runs leave no gaps in envelope coverage

steps.action: For each completed envelope, call GET /restapi/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/envelopes/{envelopeId}/documents/certificate to download the Certificate of Completion PDF into the evidence repository
steps.audit_note: The API-delivered certificate is identical to the console download and is DocuSign's authoritative signing record — the strongest evidence artefact this system produces

steps.action: Schedule the collection daily via cron or a CI/CD pipeline, with error handling that logs failures and alerts the compliance team
steps.security_note: Rotate the integration key's RSA keypair on your credential rotation schedule and remove unused consents
steps.audit_note: Failed runs should trigger re-collection within your defined SLA

steps.action: Validate each run by comparing the number of certificates collected against the envelope count returned by the list call — flag any mismatch or a volume drop below baseline
steps.audit_note: Count reconciliation provides assurance of collection completeness

L4.title: DocuSign Managed Evidence Pipeline with Connect and Monitor

steps.action: In DocuSign Admin, navigate to Integrations > Connect and add a custom Connect configuration that pushes envelope lifecycle events (sent, delivered, completed, declined, voided) to your webhook listener
steps.permissions_required: DS Admin (Connect must be enabled on the account)
steps.security_note: Serve the listener over TLS 1.2+ and enable HMAC message signing in the Connect configuration; verify the HMAC signature on every delivery

steps.action: Stream security and account events by polling GET https://lens.docusign.net/api/v2.0/datasets/monitor/stream (DocuSign Monitor API) with cursor-based paging into your SIEM or evidence platform
steps.permissions_required: JWT Grant impersonating an Organization Administrator or Security Reports Administrator; requires a DocuSign Monitor subscription
steps.security_note: Monitor is an add-on — if your account lacks it, fall back to daily eSignature REST API collection (L3) plus Connect webhooks for lifecycle coverage

steps.action: Persist the Monitor endCursor checkpoint after every successful poll and resume from it, so restarts never skip or replay events
steps.audit_note: Cursor checkpointing is the completeness guarantee for the Monitor stream — losing it creates evidentiary gaps

steps.action: Reconcile daily: compare envelopes received via Connect against a GET /restapi/v2.1/accounts/{accountId}/envelopes poll for the same window, and flag any envelope missing a stored Certificate of Completion
steps.audit_note: Webhooks can be dropped; reconciliation against the REST API is what makes the pipeline defensible as complete

steps.action: Publish a collection-health dashboard: Connect deliveries/day, Monitor events/day, reconciliation mismatches, certificate coverage %, and p95 ingestion latency, with alerts on gaps or failed runs
steps.audit_note: The dashboard itself is continuous evidence of a managed collection process

steps.action: Run an automated quarterly review that produces a collection-health report, confirms HMAC keys and RSA keypairs were rotated, and flags any SLA breaches
steps.security_note: Include a check that the Connect listener endpoint and Monitor service user consents are still restricted to authorised infrastructure
